title: ulmaria palustris var. glauca
intro: Ulmaria palustris var. glauca, also known as marshmallow or marsh mallow, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the mallow family. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and soothing effects. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in mucilages, which help to calm irritated tissues and provide relief from digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, offering various health benefits such as reducing inflammation and soothing coughs. Herbal preparations of Ulmaria palustris var. glauca can be consumed as teas, infusions, or capsules, and can also be applied topically as creams or ointments.
